missables: Wantz — speak to him on the Mountain Trail during this visit. If missed, he will NOT set up his shop later (exclusive 4-slot equipment).
sgTip: Steal Lv. 3 Key Spheres from Biran and Yenke (4–5 if possible) — they're rare. Kimahri should also learn Mighty Guard and Bad Breath from fiends on this mountain.

subLocations.name: Mountain Trail
subLocations.prose: The Ronso gatekeepers stand down after Yuna impresses them. Talk to Wantz near the start of the mountain trail — if you skip him here, his shop at Macalania (with exclusive 4-slot equipment) never appears. Primer XXV is tucked behind a rock pillar on the right side of the trail as you ascend. Later sections of the trail are plagued by Grendels and Behemoths — both hit extremely hard, can inflict Confuse, and have large HP pools, making this the toughest random encounter gauntlet outside Sin. The Ronso fiends early on are worth a Lancet from Kimahri — Gagazet Mountain Trail enemies carry a variety of Blue Magic abilities including useful status skills.

items.name: 20,000 Gil
items.notes: Chest north of first ledge — climb snowy slope, travel south to reach it

items.name: Mega-Potion x2
items.notes: Barely visible chest on west side of trail heading north

items.name: Braska's Sphere
items.notes: Short narrow path north around a large rock — counts as Jecht/Braska sphere #9 for Auron's Overdrive unlock

items.name: Defending Bracer
items.notes: T-intersection — go straight (east) before heading north

items.name: HP Sphere
items.notes: Descend slope after Wantz, look under path — first of two chests

items.name: Lv. 4 Key Sphere
items.notes: Descend slope after Wantz, look under path — second of two chests

subLocations.name: Mountain Trial / Prominence
subLocations.prose: This is a tough stretch — large maps, frequent encounters, and sparse save points. After the Biran & Yenke fight, check behind the eastern Save Sphere for the Saturn Crest (Kimahri's Spirit Lance) before moving on. The half-submerged cavern section splits Tidus, Wakka, and Rikku from the others for the underwater trials — this sub-group faces several fiend groups solo, so make sure all three have decent equipment before entering the mountain. If you have access to Rikku's Hyper Mighty G mix (Gambler's Spirit + Healing Spring), using it before Seymour Flux grants Haste, Protect, and Shell to the whole party and significantly eases the fight. After completing both trials, loot the cavern chests before taking the stone stairway out.

items.name: Lv. 1 Key Sphere
items.notes: 1st Trial reward — time Wakka's throw to hit spinning shield opening

items.name: Return Sphere
items.notes: Aquatic passage east of Save Sphere

items.name: Recovery Ring
items.notes: Aquatic passage east of Save Sphere

items.name: Saturn Crest (Kimahri's Spirit Lance)
items.notes: Hidden chest near eastern Save Sphere after defeating Seymour Flux — easy to miss while rushing; required for Caladbolg

bosses.strategy: Kimahri solo vs Biran (HP: scales) and Yenke (HP: scales) — both copy any Ronso Rage abilities Kimahri has already learned, and their stats scale to match his, so you cannot outlevel this fight. Steal Lv. 3 Key Spheres from Biran. Focus fire on one at a time — guard/defend on one while attacking the other; when side by side they protect each other, so use Lancet, Overdrives, or wait for them to split. Use Lancet on Biran to learn Mighty Guard (he uses it near death) — this is the correct chapter for that, not Defender X in the Calm Lands. Use Lancet on Yenke to learn White Wind. Biran may cast Berserk on Kimahri — have a backup character ready with Esuna, or use Remedies. When one falls the survivor goes berserk and hits harder, so heal up before landing the killing blow.

Seymour Flux (70,000 HP) + Mortiorchis (4,000 HP). Keep the entire party above ~2,500 HP at all times — Mortiorchis charges Total Annihilation over two to three turns (telegraphed by the 'Auto-attack Mode' message), which hits the full party for massive damage. Kill Mortiorchis immediately to interrupt the charge before it fires. Silence Seymour to shut down his magic; if Silence misses he gains Protect and Reflect, bouncing Flare at random characters — Dispel both immediately. Lance of Atrophy inflicts Zombie on one character and is followed by Full-Life — swap that character out instantly, or cure with Holy Water (Esuna does NOT work). Arrive with full Overdrives. Steal Elixirs from Seymour.

HP: 40,000. Mana Breath drains MP from whoever it hits — bring Ethers and swap in a character with a full MP pool if a caster runs dry. Photon Spray hits the whole party for moderate damage; summon an aeon right after to absorb follow-up hits in your place. Tail Sweep knocks out one row, so watch positioning. Critical warning: below 50% HP, Sanctuary Keeper casts Curaga on itself — cast Reflect on it beforehand to bounce that Curaga back at the party as free healing. Attack name is Photon Wings (hits the whole party and inflicts random status ailments) — have Remedies ready. Open with Protect and Haste, keep Yuna free to heal, and push hard before MP attrition becomes a problem.
